name: Hot-Sauce Chili
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds lean boneless beef chuck roast, well trimmed and cut into 1-inch cubes
4 1/2 ounces canned chopped green chilies, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat the oil over medium-high heat; add the beef and cook for 5 minutes.
2. Drain off the liquid, then add the onion and garlic. Sauté for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the beef is browned on all sides and the onion is tender.
3. Add the chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper sauce; cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
4. Add the water and chilies and bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Uncover and simmer for another 45 minutes or until the beef is fork-tender, stirring occasionally.
Notes: Serve over rice and garnish with chopped onions, shredded cheese, and sour cream, if desired.
